sources.snippet: Musk is now worth $1.11tn according to the Bloomberg rich list, while SpaceX listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange with a value of $2.2tn.
sources.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX soars in stock market debut

sources.snippet: The launch was smoother than many observers expected, with trading kicking off late on Friday morning without any hiccups.
sources.title: SpaceX surges past $2 trillion in Nasdaq debut, closes in on Amazon

sources.snippet: As of today, SpaceX is owned by investors who will want to see it make money.
sources.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
Retail investors build big dreams on small slices of SpaceX

topic.urgencyReason: IPO completed today, making Musk world's first trillionaire; immediate market impact.

enriched.summary:
- SpaceX's initial public offering (IPO) on the Nasdaq on June 13, 2026, propelled Elon Musk to become the world's first trillionaire, as shares began trading at a valuation exceeding $1 trillion.
- The IPO marks a historic milestone in financial markets, with SpaceX's valuation driven by its dominance in commercial spaceflight, Starlink satellite internet, and government contracts.
- Main countries/regions involved: United States (Nasdaq listing, SpaceX headquarters, primary market), with global investor participation.
